description: Add comprehensive E2E tests for the permission confirmation dialog system covering all untested critical and high-severity scenarios: multiple pending confirmations with prev/next navigation, dialog persistence across side panel close/reopen, escape/outside-click blocking, parameters display, plugin tool confirmation (not just browser tools), Always Allow switch reset behavior, and badge count for multiple confirmations.

userStories.title: E2E test: multiple pending confirmations with prev/next navigation
userStories.description: Test that when multiple 'ask' tools are called concurrently, the dialog shows 'X of Y' counter, prev/next buttons work correctly, and approving/denying individual items updates the counter and resolves only that specific tool call.
acceptanceCriteria: Two concurrent tool calls with 'ask' permission trigger a dialog showing '1 of 2'
Clicking 'next' navigates to the second confirmation ('2 of 2')
Clicking 'prev' navigates back to the first confirmation ('1 of 2')
'prev' button is disabled when at index 0
'next' button is disabled when at the last index
Clicking 'Allow' on one confirmation resolves that specific tool call and updates the counter
After approving the first, the dialog shows the remaining confirmation without 'X of Y' (count=1)
Approving the second confirmation resolves the remaining tool call and closes the dialog
Badge shows '2' during both pending, then '1', then clears

userStories.title: E2E test: confirmation dialog survives side panel close and reopen
userStories.description: Test that closing the side panel while a confirmation dialog is showing, then reopening the side panel, correctly re-hydrates the pending confirmation from the background script.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool call with 'ask' permission triggers the dialog
Side panel is closed (page.close())
Side panel is reopened (openSidePanel)
The confirmation dialog reappears with the correct tool info
Clicking Allow in the reopened dialog resolves the original tool call
The MCP client receives the tool result successfully

userStories.title: E2E test: escape key and outside click do not dismiss the confirmation dialog
userStories.description: Test that pressing Escape or clicking outside the confirmation dialog does NOT dismiss it, verifying the preventDefault handlers on onEscapeKeyDown and onInteractOutside.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Escape while the dialog is open does NOT close it
The dialog remains visible after Escape
The tool call is still pending (not denied) after Escape
Clicking outside the dialog does NOT close it
The dialog must still be manually Allow-ed or Deny-ed

userStories.title: E2E test: confirmation dialog shows parameters and they are expandable
userStories.description: Test that when a tool is called with parameters, the confirmation dialog shows an expandable 'Parameters' details element containing the correct JSON-formatted params.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool call with parameters triggers dialog showing 'Parameters' summary
Clicking the 'Parameters' summary expands the details element
The expanded content shows the correct JSON-formatted params
Tool calls without parameters do NOT show the Parameters section

userStories.title: E2E test: plugin tool confirmation dialog (not just browser tools)
userStories.description: Test that the confirmation dialog works correctly for plugin tools (e.g., e2e-test plugin), not just browser tools, verifying the plugin dispatch path handles 'ask' permission correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Setting e2e-test plugin permission to 'ask' triggers a confirmation dialog when calling e2e_test_echo
The dialog shows the correct tool name (e2e_test_echo) and plugin name
Clicking Allow completes the tool and returns the correct result
Clicking Deny returns PERMISSION_DENIED error
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/permissions.e2e.ts (add new test)

Context:
All existing confirmation dialog E2E tests use browser tools (`browser_list_tabs`). Browser tools use `handleBrowserToolCall` in `mcp-tool-dispatch.ts`, while plugin tools use `handlePluginToolCall`. Both call the shared `runAskFlow`, but the surrounding dispatch logic differs — plugin tools require an active tab with a ready adapter.

To test plugin tools, the e2e-test plugin must have a matching tab that is ready. The test needs:
1. Start the test-server (provides the matching web page)
2. Open a tab navigating to the test-server URL
3. Wait for the e2e-test plugin to become ready
4. Set e2e-test plugin permission to 'ask'
5. Call `e2e_test_echo` with params
6. Dialog should appear — Allow or Deny

The existing `testServer` fixture starts a test server. Use it along with the e2e-test plugin.

Set permissions:
```json
{
  "e2e-test": { "permission": "ask" }
}
```

Call the tool:
```typescript
mcpClient.callTool('e2e_test_echo', { message: 'hello' }, { timeout: 35_000 })
```

The dialog should show:
- Tool: `e2e_test_echo`
- Plugin: `E2E Test` (or `e2e-test`)
- Parameters: `{ "message": "hello" }`

For the Allow test, verify the result contains the echoed message.
For the Deny test, verify the error contains 'denied by the user'.

This test needs the test server + tab navigation, which is more involved than the browser tool tests. Follow the pattern from `side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts` for the test server setup.

Constraints:
- Use the existing `testServer` fixture from the PermissionFixtures.
- Navigate a tab to the test server URL to make the plugin ready.
- Wait for the plugin to reach 'ready' state before calling the tool.
- Add in a new `test.describe('Confirmation dialog — plugin tools')` block.

userStories.title: E2E test: Always Allow switch resets after each Allow/Deny decision
userStories.description: Test that the 'Always allow this tool' switch resets to unchecked (false) after each Allow or Deny action, so the next confirmation in the queue starts fresh.
acceptanceCriteria: After clicking Allow (without toggling Always Allow), the switch is unchecked for the next confirmation
After toggling Always Allow and clicking Allow, the switch is unchecked for the next confirmation
After clicking Deny, the switch is unchecked for the next confirmation

userStories.title: E2E test: badge count for multiple pending confirmations
userStories.description: Test that the Chrome extension badge correctly shows the count for 2+ pending confirmations, increments and decrements as confirmations are resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: Badge shows '2' when two 'ask' tool calls are pending
After Allow-ing one, badge shows '1'
After Allow-ing the second, badge clears to ''
Badge shows '3' when three 'ask' tool calls are pending simultaneously
